steps.title: Identify what the example needs
steps.content: The passage argues world's fairs are "too expensive and not popular enough." The example cites the 1984 World's Fair costing 350 million dollars, then needs an attendance figure from the table to complete the claim of low popularity. The correct figure must come from the 1984 row.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
Match each attendance figure to its year in the table:
  • Choice A — 7.35 million visitors.: The table shows the 1984 World's Fair had exactly 7.35 million visitors — this is the figure that completes the 1984 example. ✓
  • Choice B — 9.60 million visitors.: 9.60 million comes from a different year in the table, not 1984 — this misrepresents the data. ✗
  • Choice C — 6.40 million visitors.: 6.40 million comes from a different year in the table, not 1984 — this misrepresents the data. ✗
  • Choice D — 5.60 million visitors.: 5.60 million comes from a different year in the table, not 1984 — this misrepresents the data. ✗
A is correct.
